items.headline: Leonard’s 34-point night lifts Clippers over Mavericks after blown 23-point lead
items.blurb: Leonard’s 34-point night lifts Clippers over Mavericks after blown 23-point lead — Kawhi Leonard scored 34 points, hitting six 3-pointers, and the Los Angeles Clippers recovered after blowing a 23-point lead to beat rookie Cooper Flagg and the Dallas Mavericks 116-103 on Tuesday night.

items.headline: Heat stumble into play-in for fourth straight season
items.blurb: Heat stumble into play-in for fourth straight season — Not only did the Miami Heat lose Tuesday for the ninth time in their last dozen games, but the defeat sent them into the play-in tournament for the fourth season in a row.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Clippers 116, Mavericks 103
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Clippers 116, Mavericks 103 — Kawhi Leonard tallied 34 points and Darius Garland added 22 as the Los Angeles Clippers took down the Dallas Mavericks 116-103.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Thunder 123, Lakers 87
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Thunder 123, Lakers 87 — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander poured in 25 points and Isaiah Joe added 18 off the bench as the Oklahoma City Thunder blew out the shorthanded Los Angeles Lakers 123-87.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Warriors 110, Kings 105
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Warriors 110, Kings 105 — De’Anthony Melton and Brandin Podziemski combined for 41 points, each going 4-for-6 from three, while Steph Curry added 17 off the bench as the Golden State Warriors ground out a 110–105 win over the Sacramento Kings.

items.headline: Lakers legend Michael Cooper has landed a college basketball head coaching job
items.blurb: Lakers legend Michael Cooper has landed a college basketball head coaching job — Michael Cooper, a five-time NBA champion as a member of the Los Angeles Lakers, is now a head coach in college basketball.

items.headline: Raptors gain ground in East playoff race with convincing win over Heat
items.blurb: Raptors gain ground in East playoff race with convincing win over Heat — Tuesday’s win over the Heat was a moment worth savouring for the Raptors. They jumped all over the Heat for a comfortable win that helped them gain a one-game advantage for sixth place in the East.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Celtics 113, Hornets 102
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Celtics 113, Hornets 102 — Jaylen Brown had 35 points, and Jayson Tatum chipped in 23 as the Boston Celtics beat the Charlotte Hornets 113-102.

items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Pelicans 156, Jazz 137 — Jeremiah Fears set a franchise rookie-record with 40 points, and Derik Queen supplied 17 points and 12 rebounds as the New Orleans Pelicans won a shootout over the Utah Jazz 156-137.

items.blurb: Adama Bal, Grizzlies Sign 2nd 10-Day Contract — The Memphis Grizzlies have signed forward Adama Bal to a second 10-Day contract.
